principle: A later key is evaluated only when every earlier key is equal and fully known for both candidates.

keys.expression: 0 if crocoburio_advance, 1 if neutral, 2 if dragon_advance, null if unknown
0 if crocoburio_win, 1 if nonterminal, 2 if unknown, 3 if dragon_win
negative of the ascending per-spell post-turn resource vector; numeric values are capped at known max, availability-only maps available=1 unavailable=0
negative count of orthogonally adjacent walkable_confirmed or walkable_observed cells at finalCell
negative minimum confidence of player, used pillars/cells, glyph pattern and calibration
lexicographic comparison of action tuples [spellOrdinal,targetKindOrdinal,x,y,pillarId], repeated per action; empty sequence is []

sequenceKeyFormat: Display signature remains <spell>@<target-kind>:<x>,<y>:<pillar-id-or-dash>; comparison uses parsed typed tuples, not raw string ordering.
notes: Pillar-based next-turn mobility is not ranked because pillar positions change each turn.
Unknown future resources block only when the resource key is reached after all earlier keys tie.
